title: Solihull (UK Parliament constituency)

infobox.value: [Solihull West and Shirley](/wiki/Solihull_West_and_Shirley_(UK_Parliament_constituency) "Solihull West and Shirley (UK Parliament constituency)")

was a [constituency](/wiki/Constituencies_of_the_Parliament_of_the_United_Kingdom "Constituencies of the Parliament of the United Kingdom") in [West Midlands](/wiki/West_Midlands_(county) "West Midlands (county)") represented in the [House of Commons](/wiki/House_of_Commons_of_the_United_Kingdom "House of Commons of the United Kingdom") of the [UK Parliament](/wiki/Parliament_of_the_United_Kingdom "Parliament of the United Kingdom").
The part of the County Borough of Birmingham in the present Tamworth constituency, and the urban district of Solihull.[[5]](#cite_note-5)

The Metropolitan Borough of Solihull wards of [Elmdon](/wiki/Elmdon,_West_Midlands "Elmdon, West Midlands"), Lyndon, Olton, St Alphege, Shirley East, Shirley South, Shirley West, and [Silhill](/wiki/Silhill "Silhill").

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Parliamentary constituency in the United Kingdom, 1945-2024

| Solihull | |
| --- | --- |
| Former [borough constituency](/wiki/Borough_constituency "Borough constituency") for the [House of Commons](/wiki/House_of_Commons_of_the_United_Kingdom "House of Commons of the United Kingdom") | |
| [Outline map](/wiki/File:Solihull2007Constituency.svg) 2010–2024 boundary of Solihull in [West Midlands](/wiki/West_Midlands_(county) "West Midlands (county)") | |
| [Outline map](/wiki/File:EnglandWestMidlandsCounty.svg) Location of [West Midlands](/wiki/West_Midlands_(county) "West Midlands (county)") within England | |
| [County](/wiki/Ceremonial_counties_of_England "Ceremonial counties of England") | [West Midlands](/wiki/West_Midlands_(county) "West Midlands (county)") |
| [Electorate](/wiki/Electoral_registration_in_the_United_Kingdom "Electoral registration in the United Kingdom") | 77,354 (December 2010)[[1]](#cite_note-1) |
| Major settlements | [Solihull](/wiki/Solihull "Solihull") |
| [1945](/wiki/1945_United_Kingdom_general_election "1945 United Kingdom general election")–[2024](/wiki/2024_United_Kingdom_general_election "2024 United Kingdom general election") | |
| Seats | One |
| Created from | [Tamworth](/wiki/Tamworth_(UK_Parliament_constituency) "Tamworth (UK Parliament constituency)") |
| Replaced by | [Solihull West and Shirley](/wiki/Solihull_West_and_Shirley_(UK_Parliament_constituency) "Solihull West and Shirley (UK Parliament constituency)") |

**Solihull** was a [constituency](/wiki/Constituencies_of_the_Parliament_of_the_United_Kingdom "Constituencies of the Parliament of the United Kingdom") in [West Midlands](/wiki/West_Midlands_(county) "West Midlands (county)") represented in the [House of Commons](/wiki/House_of_Commons_of_the_United_Kingdom "House of Commons of the United Kingdom") of the [UK Parliament](/wiki/Parliament_of_the_United_Kingdom "Parliament of the United Kingdom").

Further to the completion of the [2023 Periodic Review of Westminster constituencies](/wiki/2023_Periodic_Review_of_Westminster_constituencies "2023 Periodic Review of Westminster constituencies"), the seat was abolished and its area was split. The bulk of the constituency was reformed as **[Solihull West and Shirley](/wiki/Solihull_West_and_Shirley_(UK_Parliament_constituency) "Solihull West and Shirley (UK Parliament constituency)")**, first contested at the [2024 general election](/wiki/2024_United_Kingdom_general_election "2024 United Kingdom general election").[[2]](#cite_note-2) Other parts of the constituency moved into the new seat of [**Meriden and Solihull East**](/wiki/Meriden_and_Solihull_East_(UK_Parliament_constituency) "Meriden and Solihull East (UK Parliament constituency)").[[3]](#cite_note-3)

## Constituency profile

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Solihull_(UK_Parliament_constituency)&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Constituency profile")]

The Solihull area is home to some of the West Midlands's more affluent residents and includes a high proportion of Birmingham workers and the managerial classes in manufacturing, retail, industry and the public sector. There are smaller villages and undeveloped green belt areas in its peripheral countryside, though the seat was primarily suburban and middle-class, with low levels of deprivation throughout. Workless claimants stood at only 2% of the population in November 2012, below every regional average in the UK. In the study of that date, only three of the 59 West Midlands seats had a lower proportion of registered jobseekers.[[4]](#cite_note-4)

Following boundary changes, the northernmost tip of the seat contained the point in England furthest from the coast in any direction.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

## Boundaries

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Solihull_(UK_Parliament_constituency)&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Boundaries")]

![Map](https://maps.wikimedia.org/img/osm-intl,a,a,a,300x200.png?lang=en&domain=en.wikipedia.org&title=Solihull_%28UK_Parliament_constituency%29&revid=1311736637&groups=_82ab09b8e458b62703f95932a20f0cb7613a75f6&parser=legacy)

Map of boundaries 1983–2024

The constituency was one of two covering the [Metropolitan Borough of Solihull](/wiki/Metropolitan_Borough_of_Solihull "Metropolitan Borough of Solihull"). It covered the town of [Solihull](/wiki/Solihull "Solihull") itself, as well as [Shirley](/wiki/Shirley,_West_Midlands "Shirley, West Midlands") and [Olton](/wiki/Olton "Olton"). It is a largely well-off, residential area, in the south-east of the [West Midlands conurbation](/wiki/West_Midlands_conurbation "West Midlands conurbation").

**1945–1950**: The part of the County Borough of Birmingham in the present Tamworth constituency, and the urban district of Solihull.[[5]](#cite_note-5)

**1950–1974**: The Urban District of Solihull.[[6]](#cite_note-6)

**1974–1983**: The County Borough of Solihull.

**1983–2024**: The Metropolitan Borough of Solihull wards of [Elmdon](/wiki/Elmdon,_West_Midlands "Elmdon, West Midlands"), Lyndon, Olton, St Alphege, Shirley East, Shirley South, Shirley West, and [Silhill](/wiki/Silhill "Silhill").

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Solihull_(UK_Parliament_constituency)&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: History")]

[Conservative](/wiki/Conservative_Party_(UK) "Conservative Party (UK)") candidates won the seat from its outset in 1945 until a loss in 2005, the seat meanwhile seeing boundary changes covered above. In the [2005 general election](/wiki/2005_United_Kingdom_general_election "2005 United Kingdom general election") Solihull was won by the [Liberal Democrats](/wiki/Liberal_Democrats_(UK) "Liberal Democrats (UK)"), with [Lorely Burt](/wiki/Lorely_Burt "Lorely Burt") beating the incumbent [John Taylor](/wiki/John_Mark_Taylor "John Mark Taylor") by a majority of 279 votes. Burt won the seat again at the 2010 general election, this time by just 175 votes following two recounts.

The seat was represented by [Julian Knight](/wiki/Julian_Knight_(politician) "Julian Knight (politician)") since 2015, who won the seat from Burt with a majority of 12,902. At the 2017 election, Knight increased his majority to just over 20,000, with a similar result in 2019, making Solihull a [safe](/wiki/Safe_seat "Safe seat") Conservative seat.

However, following allegations of serious sexual assault made to the [Metropolitan Police](/wiki/Metropolitan_Police "Metropolitan Police") against Knight in December 2022, Knight sat as an [independent](/wiki/Independent_politician "Independent politician") MP, having had the Conservative [whip](/wiki/Whip_(politics) "Whip (politics)") suspended.[[7]](#cite_note-:0-7)

Although its predecessor seat was won by the Liberal Democrats in 2005, [intervening boundary changes](/wiki/Fifth_Periodic_Review_of_Westminster_constituencies "Fifth Periodic Review of Westminster constituencies") made the constituency notionally Conservative prior to the 2010 general election, and it is therefore listed as a **gain** rather than a hold.
